channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Florida farmers struggle to adapt as disease and climate take toll on citrus industry
item.description: Florida's citrus industry is in deep decline and growers are trying to hang on as they find ways to withstand disease and disasters.
item.content:encoded: Florida's citrus industry is in deep decline and growers are trying to hang on as they find ways to withstand disease and disasters.
item.dc:creator: Tom Hudson

item.title: Looking back at Jerome Powell's 8-year term as Federal Reserve Chair
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Patrick Harker, former president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia, about Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's tenure at the central bank, which ends this week.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Patrick Harker, former president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia, about Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's tenure at the central bank, which ends this week.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: They graduate to six figure salaries, and grueling work
item.description: Cadets from the nation's Merchant Marine academies are finding lots of demand and great salaries because of a shortage of licensed mariners.
item.content:encoded:
Cadets from the nation's Merchant Marine academies are finding lots of demand and great salaries because of a shortage of licensed mariners.

(Image credit: SUNY Maritime College)
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: ABC argues Trump administration is trying to chill free speech
item.description: In a filing, ABC accuses the Trump administration of trying to chill its constitutionally protected free speech. The point of contention: "The View," and whether it's subject to equal time rules.
item.content:encoded:
In a filing, ABC accuses the Trump administration of trying to chill its constitutionally protected free speech. The point of contention: "The View," and whether it's subject to equal time rules.

(Image credit: Gabriela Passos)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Prices are up, but Mother's Day still means brunch
item.description: It's the biggest day for America's restaurants, and one of the biggest for flower sales. Despite anxieties over rising gas prices, people are ready to splurge to celebrate.
item.content:encoded:
It's the biggest day for America's restaurants, and one of the biggest for flower sales. Despite anxieties over rising gas prices, people are ready to splurge to celebrate.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: With Spirit in liquidation, here's what happens next to its planes
item.description: When Spirit Airlines stopped flying last weekend, it left more than 90 planes scattered across the country. Many will go back to the companies that own them, while Spirit tries to monetize the rest.
item.content:encoded:
When Spirit Airlines stopped flying last weekend, it left more than 90 planes scattered across the country. Many will go back to the companies that own them, while Spirit tries to monetize the rest.

(Image credit: Giorgio Viera)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Trade court strikes down a second round of Trump tariffs
item.description: The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.
item.content:encoded:
The Court of International Trade has struck down a second round of global tariffs ordered by President Trump, after his earlier import taxes were outlawed by the U.S. Supreme Court.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: She helps families in need. As gas and grocery prices rise, she needs help, too
item.description: She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.
item.content:encoded:
She has spent decades helping others struggling to make ends meet. Now the rising cost of gas and groceries has left Dalene Basden feeling the strain herself.

(Image credit: Tovia Smith/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Gas prices keep rising, but do big oil companies plan to drill more? Not so far
item.description: The war in Iran has pushed global oil prices higher, which boosts oil company revenues. But major U.S. oil companies aren't signaling plans to increase production to bring down prices at the pump.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran has pushed global oil prices higher, which boosts oil company revenues. But major U.S. oil companies aren't signaling plans to increase production to bring down prices at the pump.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Campaign staffers tell NPR they make 'thousands' betting on their own candidates
item.description: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.content:encoded: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett
